Each listed script has an XML link; opening it lets you save the script locally, edit it, and later re-upload the updated version via “Import IEG Script.” This section is part of IEG administration for accessing and managing scripts.

children.title: 8.3 Removing an Existing Script
children.summary: Explains how to remove an existing IEG script from the administration list. Each script has a Delete link that opens a confirmation dialog before permanent removal. Deleted scripts cannot be recovered or accessed again, so deletion should be used only in exceptional cases, ideally after downloading the latest version first.

children.title: 8.4 Running a Script
children.summary: This section explains how to test-run an existing IEG script from the administration list. It describes using the “Run” link, selecting a Datastore schema that matches the script’s data structure and types, and then clicking “run script” to launch the IEG Player. It also notes that any data elements saved to or retrieved from the Datastore must be defined in the chosen schema, and that missing schemas should be uploaded through Datastore administration.

children.title: 8.5 Validating a Script
children.summary: Explains how to validate an IEG script from the IEG Scripts page using the Validate link, including selecting a datastore schema and interpreting validation results. Lists common validation failures such as mismatched list question IDs, missing parent-child relationships or entities, duplicate page IDs, and missing Person/Relationship requirements for relationship summary pages. Also briefly covers uploading/importing a new script, overwriting existing scripts, and read-only mode behavior for reviewing scripts without editing.

children.title: 8.6 Uploading a New Script
children.summary: Describes how to import/upload a new IEG script from the IEG Scripts page using the “Import IEG Script” link. The upload screen requires selecting the script file and entering a logical name; the script’s id, version, and type are read from the file. If the uploaded script is meant to replace an existing script with the same id, the Overwrite checkbox must be selected or an error occurs.

children.title: 8.7 Read-only Mode
children.summary: Read-only mode makes all pages immutable during a script execution, letting users (typically caseworkers) review the script via navigation buttons and the sections panel without editing. The mode is enabled by setting the script execution’s read-only flag to true via the public API. If switched back to editable mode, resumed editing returns to the last page the editor was on, not the page the reviewer last visited.

children.title: 8.8 Command Line Development Options
children.summary: Describes the command-line Import IEG Script option for developers as an alternative to uploading scripts through administration screens. Syntax: `appbuild ieg.importscript -Dscript.file=<path> -Doverwrite=<true|false> -Dgenerate.properties=<true|false>`. `overwrite` defaults to false and blocks import if the script already exists; `generate.properties` defaults to true and controls whether text elements are extracted into the Resource Store properties. If false, only the script definition is inserted.

IEG administration covers managing existing scripts and importing new ones. It explains how admin users access the IEG Scripts page, download a script’s XML, delete scripts permanently, run test executions against a chosen Datastore schema, and validate scripts for common schema/page errors. It also describes importing a new script via the UI, including overwrite behavior, read-only mode for non-editable review, and command-line import options for developers with overwrite and property-generation flags.

children.title: 9 Compliancy for IEG
9.1 Customizing IEG Scripts
Creating a Custom Copy of an IEG Script
children.summary: Explains compliant IEG script customization. Key points: customize scripts per the relevant Cúram solution/module guide; IEG resources (text, images, icons) are stored in the Resource Store and should be extracted after changes, with scripts/resources kept under source control. Recommends creating a new copy of a script and updating script ID, type, and version, especially for sub-scripts, which require parent script updates. Also notes IEG Editor edits are in-place; production changes to non-text elements should avoid active scripts, favoring copies for changes that may affect resumed executions.

children.title: Script Upgrades
children.summary: Guidelines for compliant IEG script customization and upgrades. It explains that scripts/resources may be shipped with Cúram modules, should follow module-specific customization rules, and that modified resources should be extracted from the Resource Store and kept under source control. It recommends creating a new copy of a script and updating its ID/version/type, especially for custom sub-scripts. It also warns that the IEG Editor edits scripts in place; in production, avoid in-place non-text changes to executing or resumable scripts and copy instead.

children.title: Database Representation
IEG Database Representation
children.summary: IEG scripts and application resources are stored in the database as CLOBs and BLOBs, but database builds can overwrite them from file-system DMX files. This section explains how to keep custom artifacts under source control and prepare them for the build process. It gives steps for custom IEG scripts: extract the latest IEGSCRIPTINFO.dmx, copy it to a custom directory, remove unrelated rows, ensure the script identifier matches, rename the CLOB file for readability, and update the script definition path.

children.title: APPRESOURCE Database Representation
children.summary: Application resources are stored in the APPRESOURCE database table as BLOBs and can be overwritten during database builds. Custom IEG scripts, application resources, and their DMX files should be kept on the file system under source control. For custom IEG scripts, the section explains extracting the latest IEGSCRIPTINFO.dmx, copying it to a custom component directory, keeping only the relevant row, and updating the script definition to match the custom script/CLOB name.

Explains how IEG scripts and application resources are represented in the database and how database builds can overwrite them from file-system DMX/CLOB/BLOB artifacts. Notes that IEG scripts are stored in IEGSCRIPTINFO as CLOBs and application resources in APPRESOURCE as BLOBs, so custom artifacts should be kept on the file system under source control. Includes steps to prepare a custom IEG script: extract the latest IEGSCRIPTINFO.dmx, copy it to a custom directory, keep only the relevant row, match the script identifier, rename the CLOB for readability, and update the script definition path.

children.title: Internal IDs and Script Executions
children.summary: This section explains how to prepare and rename APPRESOURCE DMX/blob files for custom IEG components, then references the datamanager_config.xml entry needed so build processes pick them up. It also describes IEG internal IDs: scripts are updated on first execution to fill missing internal-id values, and these IDs must remain consistent for resumed executions. Finally, it introduces IEG functional identifiers (FIDS), noting IEGRuntime and IEGPageNavigator as the classes used to run IEG scripts.

children.title: IEG Functional Identifiers (FIDS)
children.summary: Explains IEG script execution internals and functional identifiers. Covers how APPRESOURCE DMX/blob resources are extracted, renamed, copied, and registered in a custom component so the build database can pick them up. Describes internal IDs: they are auto-populated on first script execution, must remain consistent for resumed executions, and should match production in upgrade environments. Introduces the IEG classes that provide functional identifiers for running scripts, especially IEGRuntime and IEGPageNavigator.

Guidelines for compliant IEG script customization and upgrade. Covers when to copy scripts vs edit in place, noting non-text changes to active/resumable scripts should use a new script copy with updated ID/type/version. Explains that scripts/resources are stored in the database but can be overwritten by builds, so custom IEGSCRIPTINFO and APPRESOURCE DMX/CLOB/BLOB artifacts must be extracted, renamed, placed in custom components, and kept under source control. Also covers internal IDs, execution/resume consistency, and IEG runtime/page navigator classes.

children.title: 9.2 Public API
children.summary: IEG defines a public API intended for use in application code, with changes/removals governed by Cúram customer-impact standards. The JavaDoc is the only source for identifying public classes, interfaces, and methods. The section also warns that IEG contains non-API public classes/interfaces/methods that may change without notice; do not depend on them, call only documented methods, and avoid placing your own classes/interfaces in IEG’s packages unless JavaDoc explicitly allows it.

children.title: 9.3 Identifying the API
children.summary: This section states that the JavaDoc shipped with IEG is the authoritative source for identifying which classes, interfaces, and methods belong to the public API. It supports the earlier note that IEG has a public API used by application code, and distinguishes it from other public members that are not part of the API and may change or be removed without notice.

children.title: 9.4 Outside the API
children.summary: IEG includes some public classes, interfaces, and methods that are outside the supported public API. You must not depend on them, call methods other than those documented in the JavaDoc, or place your own classes/interfaces in IEG’s packages unless explicitly allowed. These non-API elements may change or be removed without notice. This section warns that only JavaDoc-defined public API elements are compliant and safe for application use.

children.title: 9.5 Model Customization
children.summary: IEG model files should not be customized because customization is unsupported. This section identifies the protected file types and names: .emx and .efx files, including IntelligentEvidenceGathering.emx, IEGScriptAdmin_cat.efx, and ResourceAdmin_cat.efx.

Guidelines for compliant IEG use. Covers how to customize scripts safely: prefer copying to a new script with new ID/type/version for non-text changes, keep scripts/resources under source control, and extract custom IEGSCRIPTINFO/APPRESOURCE DMX, CLOB, and BLOB artifacts into custom components. Explains internal IDs must stay consistent for resumed executions and identifies runtime/page navigation FIDs. Defines the supported public API via shipped JavaDoc, warns against using non-API classes/packages, and states that delivered .emx/.efx model files must not be customized.
